book_claims.claim: Used to make garbanzo bean bread (farinata), a gluten-free bread from Liguria, Italy
book_claims.mechanism: Gluten-free plant protein and carbohydrate source

supplementary_research.finding: Chickpea flour has a low glycemic index (~35) and high resistant starch content; its protein digestibility-corrected amino acid score (PDCAAS) is 0.71.
Farinata retains 85-90% of chickpea's soluble fiber and polyphenols after baking; its gluten-free protein matrix supports satiety and stable postprandial glucose.

culinary_pairings.tradition: Ligurian farinata baked in olive oil

nutrient_highlights.bioavailability_notes: PDCAAS 0.71; combining with grains creates complete protein
Escapes small intestine; fermented by colonic bacteria to butyrate supporting gut epithelial health
At food levels show cholesterol-lowering and anti-cancer properties

synergies.description: Oleic acid enhances absorption of chickpea's fat-soluble phytosterols; combined effect reduces LDL cholesterol
Rosemary's carnosic acid complements chickpea's isoflavones for combined antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activity
Provides complete protein and combined fiber for sustained energy and gut microbiome support
